primary_diagnosis.name: Acute systemic flare of mild mixed connective tissue disease (anti-U1 RNP), no organ involvement

pathophysiology_summary: Mixed connective tissue disease (anti-U1 RNP) overlaps features of SLE, systemic sclerosis and polymyositis; mild disease presents with puffy ('sausage') fingers, arthralgia/myalgia and profound flu-like fatigue during flares, without organ damage. The dramatic exhaustion and swelling raise fear of an organ crisis or infection, but the serious complications to screen — pulmonary (pleuritis, interstitial disease, pulmonary hypertension: breathlessness, pleuritic pain), cardiac (pericarditis, palpitations), renal crisis (hypertension), critical Raynaud's/digital ischaemia (stark white/blue painful fingers) and serious infection (high fever) — are all absent here, and can be screened by history over telehealth. Management is rest, analgesia, warmth and hydration with an organ-involvement safety-net; a low threshold for review applies because she lives alone with poor intake.

differential.evidence_basis: Puffy fingers, diffuse myalgia and flu-like fatigue in known anti-U1 RNP MCTD.
Flu-like myalgia/fatigue overlaps; no respiratory symptoms or true fever, and puffy fingers point to connective tissue disease.
Screen for pleuritic/cardiac/renal features; absent here.
Screen for critical Raynaud's/hypertensive renal crisis; absent.

No pulmonary/cardiac/fever features (DI-001), no critical Raynaud's or confusion (DI-002), puffy fingers/myalgia/fatigue in known MCTD without rash (DI-003) — a mild systemic flare without organ involvement.
No pleuritic/cardiac/renal features — organ crisis not supported (safety-net retained).
No critical Raynaud's or hypertensive crisis features.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: reassure a mild flare without organ involvement, rest/paracetamol/warmth/hydration, routine GP/rheumatology review, an organ-involvement safety-net, and a low threshold for review given she lives alone with poor intake. ED over-triage of a benign flare is unnecessary.

red_flags.description: Organ involvement/serious infection — pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness, palpitations, or high fever
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would indicate pulmonary/cardiac involvement or infection needing urgent care.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed organ crisis or sepsis.

red_flags.description: Critical Raynaud's/digital ischaemia (stark white/blue painful fingers) or confusion
red_flags.clinical_significance: Digital ischaemia or encephalopathy needs urgent care.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Digital loss or a missed cerebral event.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Escalating a benign flare to ED because the patient fears organ failure, without screening the actual organ red flags.
Reassuring without screening pulmonary/cardiac/renal features or critical Raynaud's.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Bloods (inflammatory markers, renal function, muscle enzymes) and urinalysis
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Reassure a mild flare with rest/analgesia/warmth/hydration; organ-involvement safety-net; routine GP/rheumatology review, with a low threshold for review given she lives alone with poor intake.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Over-triaging a benign flare wastes resources; missing evolving organ involvement would be dangerous — hence the retained safety-nets.
